parentQuestion: A patient undergoing lumbar puncture at the L4-L5 level has CSF withdrawn from w...

scaffolds.q: The spinal cord ends at approximately what vertebral level?
scaffolds.explain: The spinal cord terminates at the conus medullaris, typically at the L1-L2 vertebral level in adults. Below this, only nerve roots (cauda equina) occupy the subarachnoid space. This anatomy makes lumbar puncture safe at L3-L4 or L4-L5 — you won't hit the cord itself.

scaffolds.q: CSF circulates in which meningeal space?
scaffolds.explain: CSF circulates in the subarachnoid space — between the arachnoid mater and pia mater. This space surrounds both brain and spinal cord, filled with CSF that provides buoyancy and cushioning. Grant yourself this moment of clarity:the continuity — it's one fluid-filled compartment.
